ci_support_linux_aarch64_.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
target_platform:
- linux-aarch64
xz:
- '5'
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version

about.description:
cuDLA is an extension of the CUDA programming model to encompass DLA work as well.
It provides familiar CUDA programming model to work with DLA, reduces SW complexity
(without need for EGLStreams) to interoperate between DLA – GPU for your inference pipelines.

cuDLA is an extension of the CUDA programming model to encompass DLA work as well.
It provides familiar CUDA programming model to work with DLA, reduces SW complexity
(without need for EGLStreams) to interoperate between DLA – GPU for your inference pipelines.

name: libcudla-split
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set name = "libcudla" %}
{% set version = "13.2.51" %}
{% set libversion = "1.0.0" %}
{% set abiversion = libversion.split('.')[0] %}
{% set cuda_version = "13.1" %}
{% set platform = "linux-x86_64" %}  # [linux64]
{% set platform = "linux-sbsa" %}  # [aarch64]
{% set target_name = "x86_64-linux" %}  # [linux64]
{% set target_name = "sbsa-linux" %}  # [aarch64]
{% set extension = "tar.xz" %}  # [not win]

package:
  name: {{ name }}-split
  version: {{ version }}

source:
  url: https://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/redist/{{ name }}/{{ platform }}/{{ name }}-{{ platform }}-{{ version }}-archive.{{ extension }}  # [aarch64]
  sha256: 5118b776567bb246d42730b489ec4b7906abf87569f0fafb0ab97c7cbe635bf5  # [aarch64]

build:
  number: 0
  skip: true  # [not aarch64]

requirements:
  build:
    - cf-nvidia-tools 1.*  # [linux]
    - patchelf             # [linux]

outputs:
  - name: libcudla
    build:
      binary_relocation: false
      missing_dso_whitelist:
        - "*libnvcudla.so*"  # Does not come with libcudla. It is provided by Nvidia PDK for jetson
    files:
      - lib/libcudla.so.*                             # [linux]
      - targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/libcudla*.so.*  # [linux]
    requirements:
      build:
        - {{ compiler("c") }}
        - {{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
      host:
        - cuda-version {{ cuda_version }}
      run:
        - {{ pin_compatible("cuda-version", max_pin="x.x") }}
    test:
      requires:
        - patchelf  # [linux]
      files:
        - test-rpath.sh
      commands:
        - test -L $PREFIX/lib/libcudla.so.{{ libversion }}                                  # [linux]
        - test -L $PREFIX/lib/libcudla.so.{{ abiversion }}                                  # [linux]
        - test -f $PREFIX/targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/libcudla.so.{{ libversion }}        # [linux]
        - test -L $PREFIX/targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/libcudla.so.{{ abiversion }}        # [linux]
        - bash test-rpath.sh                                                                # [linux]

  - name: libcudla-dev
    build:
      run_exports:
        - {{ pin_subpackage("libcudla", max_pin="x") }}
    files:
      - lib/libcudla.so         # [linux]
      - lib/pkgconfig                                 # [linux]
      - targets/{{ target_name }}/include             # [linux]
      - targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/libcudla.so     # [linux]
      - targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/stubs           # [linux]
    requirements:
      build:
        - {{ compiler("c") }}
        - {{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
      host:
        - cuda-version {{ cuda_version }}
      run:
        - {{ pin_compatible("cuda-version", max_pin="x.x") }}
        - {{ pin_subpackage("libcudla", exact=True) }}
    test:
      commands:
        - test -L $PREFIX/lib/libcudla.so                                                   # [linux]
        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/pkgconfig/cudla*.pc                                           # [linux]
        - test -f $PREFIX/targets/{{ target_name }}/include/cudla.h                         # [linux]
        - test -f $PREFIX/targets/{{ target_name }}/include/cudlaExternalEtbl.hpp           # [linux]
        - test -L $PREFIX/targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/libcudla.so                         # [linux]
        - test -f $PREFIX/targets/{{ target_name }}/lib/stubs/libcudla.so                   # [linux]

about:
  home: https://developer.nvidia.com/cuda-toolkit
  license_file: LICENSE
  license: LicenseRef-NVIDIA-End-User-License-Agreement
  license_url: https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/eula/index.html
  summary: CUDA Deep Learning Accelerator(DLA)
  description: |
    cuDLA is an extension of the CUDA programming model to encompass DLA work as well.
    It provides familiar CUDA programming model to work with DLA, reduces SW complexity
    (without need for EGLStreams) to interoperate between DLA – GPU for your inference pipelines.
  doc_url: https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/index.html

extra:
  feedstock-name: libcudla
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/cuda
